texto: what are you looking for? There is no Truth. There's only action, action obeying a million different impulses, ephemeral action, action subjected to every possible and imaginable contingency and contradiction, Life. Life is crime, theft, jealousy, hunger, lies, disgust,stupidity, sickness, volcanic eruptions, earthquakes, piles of corpses. what can you do about it, my poor friend?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Blaise Cendrars que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “what are you looking for? There is no Truth. There's only action, action obeying a million differ…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, dostoyevsky, existentialism, com foco em termino e existentialism.

texto: For action, whatever its immediate purpose, also implies relief at doing something, anything, and the joy of exertion. This is the optimism that is inherent in, and proper and indispensable to action, for without it nothing would ever be undertaken. It in no way suppresses the critical sense or clouds the judgment. On the contrary this optimism sharpens the wits, it creates a certain perspective and, at the last moment, lets in a ray of perpendicular light which illuminates all one's previous calculations, cuts and shuffles them and deals you the card of success, the winning number.
explicacao: Citação de Blaise Cendrars sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “For action, whatever its immediate purpose, also implies relief at doing something, anything, and…” articula sucesso, action, depression em registro de sucesso, com relevância para contextos como termino e depression.

texto: ContrastsThe windows of my poetry are wide open on the boulevards and in the shop windowsShineThe precious stones of lightListen to the violins of the limousines and the xylophones of the linotypesThe sketcher washes with the hand-towel of the skyAll is color spotsAnd the hats of the women passing by are comets in the conflagration of the eveningUnity There's no more unityAll the clocks now read midnight after being set back ten minutesThere's no more time.There's no more money.In the ChamberThey are spoiling the marvelous elements of raw material("Contrasts")

texto: Modern man has a need for simplification that tends to find its expression one way or another. And this artificial monotony which he takes pains to create, this monotony which is slowly taking over the world, this monotony is the sign of our greatness. It bears the mark of a certain will-power, the will to utility; it is the expression of utility, a law that governs all our modern activity: the Law of Utility.
explicacao: Citação de Blaise Cendrars sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Modern man has a need for simplification that tends to find its expression one way or another. An…” articula reflexao, society, monotony em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e reflexao.

texto: A mud-stained sunlight began to splatter the sodden fields, and the hateful, nasal world of birds began to come to life. It seemed to me that I was coming out of a suffocating nightmare and that the low clouds flying before the wind were the shreds of an evil dream.
explicacao: Citação de Blaise Cendrars sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “A mud-stained sunlight began to splatter the sodden fields, and the hateful, nasal world of birds…” articula motivacao, bleak, dark em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao-noturna e boa-noite.
